_description: Lehmann TNBC molecular-subtype classifications mapped to DepMap ACH IDs. Two label systems carried per cell line: tnbcType6 (the original 2011 six-class scheme: BL1, BL2, IM, M, MSL, LAR) and tnbcType4 (the 2016 reclassification that collapsed IM and MSL to immune / stromal contamination signal — IM lines have null tnbcType4).
source_2011: Lehmann et al. JCI 2011 PMID 21633166, Table 4 + Supplementary Table S5 (TNBCtype-6 classifications)
source_2016: Lehmann et al. PLOS ONE 2016 PMID 27310713 (TNBCtype-4 reclassification)

subtypes_2011.BL1: Basal-like 1: cell cycle, DNA damage response, ATR/BRCA pathway. Often platinum/PARP-sensitive.
subtypes_2011.BL2: Basal-like 2: growth-factor signaling (EGF, NGF, MET, IGF1R, Wnt/β-catenin), glycolysis/gluconeogenesis. Often growth-factor-receptor-targetable.
subtypes_2011.IM: Immunomodulatory: immune-cell signaling (Th1/Th2, NK, B-cell, antigen processing). Largely an infiltrate-derived signal — re-attributed in 2016.
subtypes_2011.M: Mesenchymal: cell-motility, EMT, ECM-receptor interaction, growth-factor receptors.
subtypes_2011.MSL: Mesenchymal stem-like: shares M signature plus low proliferation, growth-factor signaling, claudin-low. Re-attributed to stromal infiltration in 2016.
subtypes_2011.LAR: Luminal androgen receptor: AR-driven, often PIK3CA mutant. Bicalutamide / enzalutamide candidate.
